A [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] '''alien species''' lived during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]]. They had dark [[Color/Legends|gray]] or dark blue skin and possessed an elongated head.<ref name="Tales 12" /> One such being was once at the [[Mrlsst Trade and Science Academy]].<ref name="X-Wing 8" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Xenomorph.jpg|thumb|right|150px|The creature's appearance in the two comics bears a strong similarity to the 'Xenomorph' from the film ''Alien''.]]
The species first appeared in [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 8|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 8]].<ref name="X-Wing 8">[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 8|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 8]]</ref> The Xenomorph of ''Alien'' featured directly in the humorous non-canon comic [[The Revenge of Tag & Bink]] where it appeared to be thumb-wrestling a member of the Yautja species from ''Predator'' in [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Cantina]].<ref name="Tales 12" />

In ''Terminators: Five Movie Monsters to Torment Your PCs'', an article published in ''[[InQuest 39]]'', [[Wizard Entertainment]] gave suggestions for how to incorporate monsters from the film [[Wikipedia:Alien (film)|''Alien'']], in which Xenomorph aliens had appeared; [[Wikipedia:Terminator (franchise)|''Terminator'']]; and other films into [[roleplaying game]] adventures. Statistics were provided for use in ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', ''Advanced Dungeons & Dragons'', and ''Vampire: The Masquerade.<ref name="InQuest 39">[[InQuest 39|''InQuest'' 39]]</ref>
